query: mechanism of bonding between zinc oxide eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Bonding
retrieved_docs.content: GIC chemically (ionic) bonds to the tooth structure (enamel and dentin). GIC bonds better to enamel than dentin because of the higher inorganic content in enamel. The mechanism of GIC adhesion to the tooth's inorganic structure involves a chelation reaction between the carboxyl groups of the polyacrylic acid and the calcium in the hydroxyapatite crystals of the tooth.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: Setting occurs by light-induced polymerization. The acid-base setting mechanism does not exist due to the absence of polyacids from the composition.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement has compressive strength equivalent to zinc phosphate cement, whereas its tensile strength is slightly higher than zinc phosphate cement. Type I GIC has lower compressive strength than Type II GIC. GIC is less stiff and has a lower modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin but lesser than zinc phosphate cement. [8] The resilience of GIC is similar to dentin, which is why it is known as man-made dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: The setting of GIC is through an acid-base reaction involving the following stages: Dissolution and decomposition: after mixing the powder and liquid, the acid dissolves the surface of the glass particles to release SiO 4 4- , Ca 2+ , Na + , and F - ions. Initial setting: the Ca 2+ released in the aqueous medium reacts with polyacrylic acid to form a 3-dimensional cross-linked structure. At this time, the material has low strength, and it can be carved using sharp instruments. Final setting: the setting reaction continues during the next 24 hours. During this time, SiO 4 4- forms the silica gel. The slow-moving Al 3+ ions enter the aqueous medium and cross-link the polyacrylic acid chains, displacing the Ca 2+ ions. This increases the final strength of the set cement. Maturation: the aluminum and calcium cross-linked polyacrylate chains get hydrated over time by absorbing water from the aqueous medium. This process is known as maturation. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Fluoride Release
retrieved_docs.content: Two mechanisms have been proposed to explain the fluoride release from GIC into an aqueous solution. [12]  The first mechanism is a short-term process involving rapidly dissolving fluorides from the outer surface into the solution. [12] The second mechanism is a continuous and gradual diffusion of fluoride through the cement.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Alginate Impressions -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Impression materials are classified as either elastic or non-elastic based on mechanical properties. [1] Elastic materials are capable of stretching, compressing, and recovering after deformation. Elastic materials comprise reversible and irreversible hydrocolloids, addition and condensation silicones, polysulfides, and polyether. [2] Nonelastic or rigid impression materials include impression compounds, zinc oxide eugenol, and impression waxes. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Structure of Set Cement
retrieved_docs.content: The cement consists of a core and matrix. The core constitutes unmixed powder particles, whereas the matrix comprises polyacid and HEMA polymer linked with hydrogen bonding.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Dispersed phase glasses: using dispersed phases of strengthening crystallites like aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, and zirconium oxide improved the strength. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Resin-based sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Based on their polymerization mechanism, resin sealants are available in either self-curing (auto-curing) or light-curing formulations. Self-cure sealants harden through a chemical reaction initiated by tertiary amines and benzoyl peroxide, whereas light-cure sealants utilize visible light to activate photo-initiators, providing a longer working time and more controlled placement. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Cermet ionomer cement: manufactured by sintering the metal and glass powders together, which helps achieve a strong metal bond to the glass. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Setting Reaction
retrieved_docs.content: Hybrid ionomers are set by a dual-reaction curing mechanism involving acid-base and polymerization reactions. When the liquid and powder are mixed, the polymerization reaction starts with chemical or light-mediated initiation. This set resin protects the ongoing acid-base setting reaction in the cement matrix from moisture contamination. This phenomenon is known as the "umbrella effect," reducing the early moisture sensitivity of GIC and providing greater initial strength. [25] The slowly proceeding acid-base reaction determines the final strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The hybrid ionomer bonds chemically to the tooth structure. However, the ionic activity is reduced due to the presence of resin. This results in a lesser bonding tendency.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Fluoride Release
retrieved_docs.content: The bulk of the GIC matrix plays a vital role in the fluoride release capacity. The fluoride release is influenced by intrinsic factors such as the composition of the matrix, setting mechanism, and fluoride content, and extrinsic factors like the pH of saliva, plaque, pellicle formation, liquid-to-powder ratio, mixing, setting and curing time, and the amount of exposed surface. Bleaching and tooth brushing do not significantly affect fluoride release. [13] The coverage of the surface of GIC with an adhesive or surface protective agent results in a 1.4 to 4-fold decrease in fluoride release.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1648
retrieved_docs.content: Lynestrenol and megestrol are both classified as members of the progestogen systemic hormonal contraceptives group. As progestogen agents administered systemically, they function within the same therapeutic class of hormonal contraceptives that rely on synthetic progestins to prevent pregnancy. Recognizing lynestrenol and megestrol as part of this contraceptive category helps clinicians understand their shared mechanism of action, potential side‑effect profiles, and appropriate use in family planning and related reproductive health care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1272
retrieved_docs.content: Ethinyl estradiol/folic acid/levonorgestrel is a combination pharmaceutical product that contains the progestin levonorgestrel. Levonorgestrel is a synthetic derivative whose parent compound is norgestrel, indicating that levonorgestrel shares structural and pharmacologic characteristics with norgestrel. Recognizing that levonorgestrel originates from norgestrel helps clinicians understand its mechanism of action and potential cross‑reactivity with other norgestrel‑based agents.

retrieved_docs.content: and as members of the imidazole‑derived gynecological anti‑infective group. Ornidazole occupies a central position, being classified as an imidazole‑derived antibacterial, a nitroimidazole‑derived antiprotozoal, and a child class of antiparasitic substances. Nitroimidazole derivatives defined as antiprotozoal agents include ornidazole, nimorazole, propenidazole, secnidazole and tinidazole; all share the nitroimidazole scaffold and are indicated for protozoal infections. Triazole‑derived gynecological anti‑infectives and antiseptics are exemplified by terconazole, which also possesses preventive activity against fungal infections. Colestipol hydrochloride is an anion‑exchange resin (iontoměniče) that binds bile acids in the gastrointestinal tract to lower lipid levels, representing a non‑antimicrobial therapeutic mechanism; chemically it belongs to the polyaminy class, linking it to compounds containing multiple amine groups.
